sections.subtitle: Match Overview and League Standings
sections.text: Brighton & Hove Albion, sitting at 10th in the league with 51 points, host Newcastle United, who are in an impressive 4th position with 62 points, on May 4, 2025, at the American Express Stadium in Falmer. This Round 35 clash promises to be a competitive encounter given the 11-point gap between the two sides. Newcastle's higher league standing suggests they might enter as favorites, but Brighton's mid-table stability could make for a tight contest. The match, officiated by Craig Pawson, who averages 3.7 yellow cards per game, could see a fair share of disciplinary actions.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these teams, the results are evenly split with 2 wins for Brighton, 2 for Newcastle, and 1 draw. Their most recent clash on October 19, 2024, saw Brighton edge out a 1-0 victory away at Newcastle, with goalkeeper Bart Verbruggen earning Player of the Match honors with an exceptional 8.9 Sofascore rating. Head-to-head trends show Brighton have been unbeaten in their last 4 encounters against Newcastle at home, often scoring first in 4 of the last 5 games. Additionally, both teams have scored in 5 of the last 6 meetings, hinting at another goal-filled encounter.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Newcastle, Alexander Isak stands out as a top performer, ranking 2nd in the league for goals scored with 22, significantly overperforming his expected goals of 19.04. Jacob Murphy also shines with 11 assists, placing him 2nd league-wide in this category, and his recent form (7.8 average rating over the last 5 matches) makes him a danger man. Bruno Guimarães, often fouled 2.9 times per game (1st in the league), could draw fouls and disrupt Brighton’s rhythm. On Brighton’s side, Danny Welbeck leads the attack with 9 goals, while Joël Veltman, fouled 2.2 times per game (3rd in the league), and Adam Webster, with a 77.3% aerial duel win rate (3rd in the league), bolster their defense.

sections.subtitle: Standout Performer in Recent Clashes
sections.text: Brighton’s goalkeeper Bart Verbruggen has been a consistent standout against Newcastle in the last 5 matchups, boasting an average Sofascore rating of 7.975. With 4.75 saves per game across his 4 starts in these fixtures, Verbruggen has often been a wall in goal, as evidenced by his Player of the Match performance in the last meeting. His ability to thwart Newcastle’s potent attack, led by Isak, could be pivotal once again in this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Statistical Insights
sections.text: Brighton have struggled defensively, going without a clean sheet in their last 5 games, while their matches often see over 2.5 goals (5 out of 5 recently). They rank 8th in the league for goals scored with 56, create big chances frequently (100, 6th in the league), and excel in aerial duels (52.3% won, 4th in the league). Newcastle, on the other hand, are in scintillating form, with over 2.5 goals in their last 7 matches and often scoring first in 8 of their last 9 games. Their goal distribution shows strength late in games, with 20.45% of goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes, which could test Brighton’s late-game resilience.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players and Squad Impact
sections.text: Brighton will miss key forward João Pedro, who has scored 10 goals this season and overperformed his expected goals of 8.9, alongside midfielder Georginio Rutter with 5 goals. Their absence could blunt Brighton’s attack against a Newcastle side missing midfielder Joelinton (4 goals) and defender Lewis Hall (4 assists). Despite these absences, Newcastle’s depth, with players like Harvey Barnes in form (7.56 average rating and 4 goals in the last 5 matches), could give them an edge in maintaining offensive output.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Matchups and Game Expectations
sections.text: Brighton’s high ranking in accurate crosses (170, 3rd in the league) and penalty area entries per game (28.59, 9th in the league) suggests they’ll look to exploit wide areas against Newcastle’s defense. Newcastle’s ability to win first halves (5 out of their last 7 games) and their pressing game could counter Brighton’s possession style, which ranks 7th with 52.68% average ball possession. With both teams scoring in 8 of Brighton’s last 10 home games and 5 of the last 6 head-to-heads, expect goals on both ends. Referee Craig Pawson’s tendency for cards (3.7 yellows per game) aligns with historical data showing over 4.5 cards in 5 of the last 7 meetings between these sides.
